### Post by K.Mandla on 2006-02-28
Hi. My only suggestion (and it's a pretty weak one) is that you might try a different default depth. (Of course, maybe you've tried that already.)

I've had a problem on some machines where the configuration utility tried to set the default depth to a level the video card couldn't handle, and as a result, it defaulted back to 640x480 at 256 color -- which looks horrible, by the way.

I see that your default depth (in the "Screen" section) is set to 16. You might try a different number there, and see if it helps. No guarantees, but it's an idea.

Back up your xorg.conf file before you start tweaking it, though.

Don't forget you can reconfigure the whole business with *sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg*. 

Good luck.

---

### Post by K.Mandla on 2006-02-28
Sorry. I know how disappointing things like that can be. 

Does this also happen in Gnome? What about XFCE? Seems like if it happens under one DE, it would also happen in another. 

Are you using the Nvidia drivers from the Ubuntu repositories or the Nvidia releases?

Could it be a legacy driver issue?
